# Class 9 {.ct}

![](../graphics/REB_Logo.png){fig-alt="Reaction Engineering Basics Logo" fig-align="center" width="200"}

## Non-Isothermal, Steady-State CSTR Analysis {.cc}

Class 9 introduces continuous stirred-tank reactors (CSTRs) and the quantitative analysis of their steady-state operation. It also illustrates the numerical solution of sets of algebraic-transcendental equations (ATEs).

## Learning Outcomes {.ch}

After completing Class 9 you should...

* Know the definition/defining equation for CSTR mole and reacting fluid energy balances, algebraic-transcendental equations (ATEs) and residual expressions.
* Understand how a numerical ATE solver works and how to use one
* Be able to
    * select and simplify the design equations for modeling a steady-state CSTR
    * select the reactor model variables to be found by solving the steady-state CSTR design equations
    * identify additional unknowns appearing in the design equations and write equations for calculating them
    * solve a set of ATEs numerically
    * write and solve any equations for calculating quantities of interest other than the reactor model variables

## Prepare {.ch}

* Read Sections 6.1 - 6.3, 6.6, Appendix D.2 and Example 6.6.2 in *REB, The Book*.
* Watch the Class 9 Preparation Video.
* Watch the Solving ATEs Video.
* Complete the Class 9 Preparation Assignment.

## Participate {.ch}

* Attend Class 9.

## Practice {.ch}

* Complete the Class 9 Practice Assignment.
* [Examples that can be used for additional practice](../examples/class_9/narratives_9.qmd).

{{< include ../includes/_bottom_license.qmd >}}
